title: podocarpus urbanii
intro: Podocarpus urbanii, also known as the Yellow Podocarpus or Yellow Yew, is a plant species native to Hawaii, classified as a coniferous evergreen tree or shrub belonging to the Podocarpaceae family. The plant is traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine, where its bark and leaves have been employed for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Podocarpus urbanii include its ability to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, while also being used to treat wounds and infections. In traditional Hawaiian herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, and salves to harness its medicinal properties.
